diagrams.title: Mobile Device Threat Landscape
diagrams.description: A visual overview of the various threat vectors targeting wireless mobile devices, including malware, phishing, man-in-the-middle attacks, unsecured Wi-Fi, and physical theft. This diagram helps security professionals understand the breadth of risks mobile devices face in enterprise environments.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the multiple attack surfaces and threat categories that wireless mobile devices are exposed to, emphasizing why specialized security controls are necessary.

diagrams.title: Mobile Device Security Architecture
diagrams.description: A layered security model showing the three-tier architecture of mobile device security: device security, network security, and application security. Each layer is depicted with its corresponding controls and technologies.
elements: Network Security Layer (VPN, Firewall, Intrusion Detection)
Application Security Layer (App Vetting, Sandboxing, Permissions)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the defense-in-depth approach required for mobile device security, showing how controls work together across different layers to protect sensitive information.

diagrams.title: Mobile Device Security Controls Matrix
diagrams.description: A matrix diagram mapping security controls to specific mobile device risks and compliance requirements. Shows the relationship between threats, controls (technical, administrative, physical), and the security objectives they address.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how organizations can systematically implement security controls to address specific mobile device risks while meeting regulatory compliance requirements.
